albums.title: EQUILIBRIVM
albums.description: Critics praise Anitta for adapting to her own scale and the zeitgeist of Brazilian pop, showcasing her evolution within the genre.

albums.title: I'm People
albums.description: M.C. Taylor's 11th LP is valued for its painstaking blend of the personal and political, creating an entirely human work comprised of complex, messy, and wonderful elements.

albums.title: Sol.hz
albums.description: This album offers a disorienting clarity, praised for sustaining a shifting field of texture where familiar musical signposts dissolve as quickly as they emerge.

albums.title: Collaborative Works
albums.description: Their collaborative music is described as a powerful brew, a lava flow of blues sensibility, and a collage of funk, soul, and jazz, resulting in some of the year's most daring music.

albums.title: Come Closer
albums.description: Critics highlight this album as a perfect collision of styles, existing in its own world with a clear sense of identity forged from distinctive musical journeys.

albums.title: In Times of Dragons

albums.description: Tori Amos is lauded for weaving the tale of a modern humanist odyssey through her latest work.

albums.title: FENIAN
albums.description: KNEECAP's album is noted for its refined sound, suggesting the group has cleaned up their act.

albums.title: Theft World
